news.summary: Owens Corning Inc. stocks (NYSE: OC) saw a decline following the release of its first-quarter 2025 financial results. The company experienced a remarkable 25% increase in net sales for the quarter, reaching $2.53 billion, surpassing the anticipated $2.51 billion. The sale of its glass reinforcements unit is on course for 2025, aligning with its strategic focus on building products. The company has now categorized the business as discontinued, with segments restructured into roofing, insulation, and doors. Segment sales breakdown: Roofing at $1.12 billion (up 2% YoY), Insulation at $909 million (down 5% YoY), and Doors at $540 million. Adjusted EBITDA rose to $565 million from $515 million, yielding a margin of 22.3% compared to 25.5% in the previous year.
news.headline: Owens Corning reports 25% YoY growth in Q1 net sales at $2.53B, adjusted EBITDA rises to $565M but margin decreases to 22.3%.

news.summary: On May 7, 2025, LPL Financial LLC revealed that financial advisors Stephanie Stewart, Debra Hedgcoth, and Madison Wentland from West Texas Investments have partnered with LPL Financial’s broker-dealer, Registered Investment Advisor (RIA), and custodial platforms. news.summary: Diversified Energy, a cryptocurrency miner, has left a natural gas-powered mining site in Elk County, Pennsylvania, known as Longhorn Pad A, reportedly abandoning unplugged wells and facing regulatory violations. The site was revived in 2022 for mining operations without an air quality permit but later obtained one in December 2023. A March 2025 inspection revealed infrastructure removal, leading to a violation notice for well abandonment. Diversified denies abandonment claims but has failed to meet obligations from a 2021 agreement to plug wells. The company's business model of acquiring old wells without a proper decommissioning plan has faced scrutiny. Diversified agreed to plug 3,000 wells by 2034 in a separate settlement and remains under regulatory scrutiny. Horton Township officials have received no updates, with generators and tanks removed without notice, raising concerns of abandonment. In a separate incident, Vilonia, Arkansas, rejected a crypto mining facility proposal amid strong opposition, following a increasing trend of scrutiny towards crypto mining initiatives in various US municipalities.
news.headline: Cryptominer abandons PA gas-powered site, leaving unplugged wells, facing regulatory issues. Failed to fulfill well-plugging commitment. Regulatory scrutiny continues. No updates from company. Localization challenges for US cities. Arkansas rejects crypto mining proposal. TX residents sue Marathon Digital over noise concerns. #CryptoMiningocrisy.

news.summary: Walt Disney Co (NYSE: DIS) announced its fiscal second-quarter 2025 results with a 7% year-on-year revenue growth to $23.62 billion, exceeding analyst expectations of $23.14 billion. The company's adjusted EPS of $1.45 surpassed the estimated $1.20. Disney's stock price increased after the earnings release. The quarter ended with 180.7 million Disney+ Core and Hulu subscriptions, up by 2.5 million from the previous quarter. Additionally, Disney reported 126.0 million Disney+ Core paid subscribers, a 1.4 million increase from the prior quarter. Entertainment revenue saw a 9% Y/Y increase to $10.68 billion, while sports revenue, mostly from ESPN, rose by 5% Y/Y to $4.53 billion. Experiences revenue, which includes Disney's theme parks and products, grew by 6% Y/Y. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Disney's Q2 results showed revenue growth of 7% to $23.62B, beating estimates; stock gained as company hits 180.7M streaming subscribers.

news.summary: Diversified Energy, a cryptocurrency miner, abandoned a natural gas-powered crypto mining site in Elk County, Pennsylvania, reportedly leaving behind unplugged wells and regulatory violations. The site, Longhorn Pad A, had been dormant for nearly a decade before Diversified revived it in 2022. The company operated without an air quality permit initially but later obtained one in December 2023. However, when inspected in March 2025, all mining infrastructure had been removed, leading to a violation notice for well abandonment. Diversified denied abandonment, claiming they may resume gas production, but they have failed to meet obligations to plug wells as part of a 2021 agreement. Environmentalists criticize the company's business model of acquiring aging wells without sufficient decommissioning plans, with plugging a single well costing over $100,000. Diversified faces regulatory scrutiny and legal settlements to plug thousands of wells. Local officials at the site expressed concern over the abrupt removal of equipment without notice. In a separate incident, the planning commission of Vilonia, Arkansas, rejected a proposal for a crypto mining facility due to resident opposition, echoing a broader trend of scrutiny towards such operations in various US municipalities.
news.headline: Crypto miner Diversified Energy abandons PA site, leaving behind violations. Reportedly failed to fulfill plugging obligations. Regulatory scrutiny increases. Mining facility rejected in Arkansas amid broader anti-crypto trend in US cities. Residents opposed due to noise. Ethereum's Pectra fork generates tension.

news.summary: A recent report titled "Global Gift Card and Incentive Card Market Intelligence and Future Growth Dynamics (Databook) - Q1 2025 Update" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. From 2020 to 2024, the market experienced strong growth, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 9.5%. This growth trend is expected to persist, with a forecasted CAGR of 7% for the market ahead.
news.headline: Global gift card market will grow by 8.5% annually to reach $614.7 billion in 2025, after achieving 9.5% CAGR during 2020-2024.
